addition.item: Auto-scan report now produces the same "Suggested next-turn prompts" the manual workflow has had since v2.3.4 — three prompts when the batch covered everything, four when more overrides remain (the extra "Continue with the next batch" prompt sits at the top; prompts 2 and 3 pick up a "then check the next batch" tail). Real finding ids are substituted into each prompt; never placeholders.

Auto-scan system prompt now explicitly forbids Claude from telling the user to "contact a developer" to apply fixes — the user has Claude available right in the session chat box and the suggested next-turn prompts tell them exactly how to ask.

change.item: Opus 4.7 is now the default model for both Scan model and Chat model in Options. Applied across config.xml and every PHP fallback path so behavior is consistent. Cost goes up ~5× per scan vs Sonnet; the Cost difference note in Options has been rewritten with "At the time of writing this" framing so users know to verify against current Anthropic pricing.

Chat agent no longer requires "type confirm to proceed" when the user has named explicit numbered finding ids — the ids ARE the confirmation, and auto-backup is the rollback safety net. The anti-ambiguous-pronoun guard from v2.3.4 is preserved (pronouns without ids still stop and ask).

Cross-locale parity sweep — three help / error strings in every locale (AUTOSCAN_NOTE, RUN_SCAN_NO_KEY, SESSION_CHAT_NOKEY) referenced the Options tab by its old internal name AI provider; brought in line with the actual Keys & tokens label. Plus the five model-related Options strings (SCAN_MODEL_DESC, CHAT_MODEL_DESC, MODEL_SONNET, MODEL_OPUS, MODEL_COST_WARNING) re-translated to reflect the new Opus default. 114 substring replacements across 15 locale files.
Components-menu label shortened from "Cybersalt Template Integrity" to "Template Integrity" across all 15 locales.

Method 1 (manual workflow) — Step 1 dashboard copy now explains that the Joomla API token can be saved in Options → Keys & tokens, and the dashboard prompt will auto-fill with it. Step 2 now makes the placeholder-replace step conditional on whether the user saved the token, and adds a new recommendation to run the manual scan in Claude Code rather than claude.ai because lower plan tiers can take longer than the browser request timeout.

Method 2 (automated workflow) — no-key copy now points at the Options button on the card (rather than the admin toolbar), names the correct fieldset, and explicitly notes the key stays in the site's own database. All 14 non-en-GB locales updated to match the en-GB rewrites.

fix.item: Disclaimer modal contrast on light-mode admins running dark-mode OSes — the popup no longer follows OS dark-mode preference, tracks only the admin template's theme attribute. Body/label/list text inside the card forced to inherit card color, scoped --bs-link-color into the card so host theme values can't leak through. Fixes unreadable "Don't show this again" label on Atum light-mode behind dark-mode OS.
"Don't show this again" checkbox row now visibly reads as a clickable control — background and border opacity bumped from 4%/8% to 6%/20% in light mode and 6%/10% to 8%/25% in dark mode.

maintenance.item: PHP minimum bumped from 8.1 to 8.3 in updates.xml. Reflects the new Cybersalt-wide baseline.

change.item: Update server moved from raw.githubusercontent.com to the Cybersalt Release Manager (cybersalt.com / com_csreleasemanager api.updatexml endpoint). Existing 2.4.0 sites discover this release through the old GitHub channel one last time; from 2.4.1 onwards they check the Cybersalt endpoint directly. SHA256 verification by Joomla's updater is unchanged.

addition.item: New plg_task_cstemplateintegrity Scheduled-Tasks plugin (group task), auto-enabled on install. One routine: cs-template-integrity Purge old file backups, with per-task params for retention window (days), always-keep-N-most-recent floor, and dry-run mode.

Default purge-backups scheduled task seeded on install with retention 30 / min_keep 5 / dry_run Off / interval 24h. Seeded unpublished — admin reviews under System / Scheduled Tasks and enables when ready. Idempotent: re-installs don't create duplicates.
ACTION_BACKUP_PURGED entries now appear in the Action log on every purge run, with the full result struct visible in the existing filterable Action log list view.

change.item: Package installer auto-enables both child plugins (webservices + task), so neither one starts disabled and requires manual flip-on after install.
Plugin language file ships a shim for the Joomla core COM_SCHEDULER_PARAMETERS_FIELDSET_LABEL key, which is missing on some 5.x en-GB packs and rendered as the raw key string on the task edit screen.

security.item: Scan prompt now forbids assumption / inference shortcuts during the review pass. A real test run flagged only 1 critical finding on a site that had 2 because Claude inferred a file's verdict from a similar-looking neighbour instead of fetching it. New non-negotiable "No-assumptions rule" requires every flagged override to be fetched and read independently; files not actually fetched must be listed as "not examined this run" with a paused-icon severity rather than silently omitted.

Findings-table completeness audit added before report publish — model must verify the table contains a row for every flagged id returned by the overrides list endpoint. Silent omissions used to read as clean and produced false negatives; the audit catches the gap before the report goes out.

Scan prompt now produces a "Suggested next-turn prompts" section at the end of every report, with three copy-paste-able prompts that name finding ids explicitly (Fix #1, #3. Mark #2, #4, #5 as checked.) instead of using pronouns. Removes the ambiguity that has caused the chat agent to act on the wrong rows.

Chat system prompt now refuses ambiguous-pronoun instructions. The agent must STOP and ask which specific finding numbers are meant whenever the user says "them all", "those", "the critical ones" etc. without concrete ids. Multi-row destructive actions also require an explicit "confirm" reply.

addition.item: Per-template rescan picker on the dashboard — modal with one checkbox per template that actually has overrides on disk.
Restore button on each row of the File backups list, with JS confirm and per-row CSRF.
Joomla-native filter bar (js-stools + Choices.js) on Action log and File backups list — search, action-type / session dropdowns, limit, clear.
Sortable column headers on Action log (Time / Action / Session) and File backups (Saved / File / Size / Session) with URL-preserved state.
Date + time on both list views — YYYY-MM-DD on top, HH:MM:SS below in muted text.
API tier note on the Method 2 (Run automated scan) card, explaining Anthropic's per-minute token-budget tiers.
"Open Options" button below the no-API-key warning, with base64-encoded return URL so save brings the user back to the dashboard.

security.item: API key fields (Anthropic key and Joomla API token) now use native HTML type="password" masking instead of CSS blur — bytes are masked the instant they're pasted, no visible flicker.

dismiss_all tool removed from the chat-with-Claude agent. The model misinterpreted "mark these 10 as checked" as "clear all 70 rows" in a real test session; bulk-clear now lives only in the dashboard's per-template rescan picker.

Absolute filesystem path removed from the override-file and core-file API responses (data.attributes.absolute) — redundant with the already-relativized "path" field; was leaking the hosting account's directory layout to anyone with a valid Joomla API token.

fix.item: Sortable column headers actually sort now. The sort link URL was double-encoded (Route::_ default xhtml + htmlspecialchars), so query params arrived as amp;view, amp;sort etc. — with no real view present, Joomla bounced to the dashboard.

Chat-with-Claude no longer 400s on "tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks immediately after". A new repair pass runs on every chat turn (before append and before save) that synthesises missing tool_result blocks, reorders them to the front of mixed user content, and merges consecutive same-role messages. Self-healing — sessions stuck on the old shape repair on the next turn.
Restoring a backup from the list view now redirects back to the list, not the detail page (which still showed the "Restore now" modal and looked like the action hadn't happened).
Empty Anthropic API key field is no longer rejected on save — config XML now allows clearing the saved key.

change.item: "Run automated scan now" button changed from blue (btn-primary) to green (btn-success) to distinguish action-buttons from copy-the-prompt buttons.

Chat-with-Claude rate-limit posture for 10K-TPM-tier Anthropic accounts: older tool payloads compressed at API-send time, per-turn tool-result byte cap (12 KB), system prompt steers toward small batches and terse replies.

v2 scan-with-Claude prompt rewrite — severity now judged relative to core (was absolute, falsely flagging every legacy override that mirrors core's intentionally-raw output), normalize-before-diff rule, alternative-layout handling, mandatory code-execution indicator scan, JSON:API response shape docs, STOP-on-write-failure rule, privilege precondition on every ALERT, framework-generated overrides (TCK/Helix/Gantry/YOOtheme) routed to REVIEW not ALERT, token-hygiene paragraph.

fix.item: Extension manager changelog modal now renders correctly. The <changelogurl> was pointing at CHANGELOG.html (human-readable HTML) instead of a Joomla-format changelog XML, so the modal opened blank when a user clicked the version badge. Both the package manifest and updates.xml now point at this file (changelog.xml), which is the schema Joomla actually parses.

language.item: Full translations for all 14 non-English languages: fr-FR, es-ES, it-IT, pt-BR, nl-NL, sv-SE, ru-RU, pl-PL, cs-CZ, tr-TR, el-GR, ja-JP, zh-CN. Each language: 246 admin strings plus 5 system/api/package/plugin language files. Formal admin-tool register. Brand names, technical acronyms, code references, URLs, and printf placeholders preserved untranslated per Joomla extension convention. Native review recommended for ja-JP, zh-CN, cs-CZ, el-GR, tr-TR.

addition.item: Multilingual support: language packs registered for all 15 of Cybersalt's standard languages (en-GB, de-DE, fr-FR, es-ES, it-IT, pt-BR, nl-NL, ru-RU, pl-PL, ja-JP, zh-CN, tr-TR, el-GR, cs-CZ, sv-SE).
German (de-DE) fully translated. Other 13 languages registered with English-fallback content; full translations followed in 2.3.1.
Method 1 / Method 2 action shortcut buttons at the top of the dashboard with anchor-jump to the relevant action card.
Clear button on every API key / token field in Options. Get token button on the Joomla API token field that opens the current admin's profile in a new tab and auto-hides once a token is saved.
Empty-state blur skip on key/token inputs so admins can see where to paste on a fresh field.
Mode-aware Rescan button (light = solid yellow with dark text; dark = outline yellow on near-black).

change.item: Dashboard restructure: status alert removed, sidebar moved to a footer, Recent sessions card removed (Sessions area covers it), Mark all as reviewed shortcut removed (Claude's dismiss_all tool covers it).
Cybersalt orange (#dc6b1a) + Bootstrap blue palette for the two action shortcut buttons. Navigation buttons neutral grey.
Top nav reordered alphabetically. Site Templates Management opens in a new tab so the dashboard stays open behind it.
Side menu reordered: Dashboard → Action log → File backups → Sessions.
Brand pass: every user-facing page title now reads "Cybersalt Template Integrity" (was "CS Template Integrity"). Code-side identifiers keep the cs short form.

security.item: H-1: Joomla API token plaintext exposure to view-tier (privilege escalation) closed — token now substituted only when current user holds write tier.
M-1: Backup restore positive allow-list (PathSafetyHelper::assertOverrideWriteAllowed) closes residual .htaccess / .user.ini / .css overwrite surface that survived v0.9.0's RCE hardening.
M-2: 4 MB upper cap on apply_fix / restore writes closes a disk-fill primitive.
M-3: Backup row size cap raised to 4 MB so apply_fix works on large overrides.
L-1: Anthropic key fingerprint switched from key-substring to SHA-256 prefix.
L-2: Web Services apply-fix validates session_id references an existing row.
L-3: simplexml_load_file passes LIBXML_NONET explicitly (defense-in-depth).
L-4: Per-user 12-scans-per-hour soft cap on Run automated scan.
L-5: Session download filename strips leading/trailing dots.

addition.item: Choose your Claude model for the automated workflow. Two new dropdowns under Options → AI provider: Scan model (default Sonnet 4.6) and Chat model (default Sonnet 4.6). All three Anthropic models selectable: Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.7.
Cost-warning note below the model dropdowns with rough per-scan dollar estimates and links to Anthropic's pricing and usage console.

change.item: Diagnostics → Run test now uses Haiku unconditionally regardless of scan/chat settings — no reason to burn Sonnet/Opus tokens on a 16-token verify call.

security.item: Model-id values from #__extensions.params are validated against a three-entry whitelist before being passed to the Anthropic API.

Anthropic API key field is now blurred by default in the Options form, with a Reveal toggle to unmask. Custom Joomla form field (ApikeyField) extends the standard text field, applies a CSS blur filter to the input. Avoids accidental shoulder-surfing exposure during screen shares, livestreams, or pair-debugging.
L-1: display.acknowledgeDisclaimer now calls PermissionHelper::requireView() for consistency with every other controller method.

L-2: Granular cstemplateintegrity.view ACL action is now actually enforced for admin browsing. Every admin view (Dashboard, Sessions, Session, Sessionform, Actions, Backups, Backup) calls PermissionHelper::requireView() at the top of display().
M-1: BackupDescriber::tidy() and ::titleCase() now htmlspecialchars their output internally — defense-in-depth against future code paths where path segments could contain HTML.

addition.item: Run automated scan button on the Dashboard (visible when an Anthropic API key is saved). One click drives the entire override review server-side: walks #__template_overrides, reads override + core file pairs, sends them to Anthropic with a structured prompt, saves the markdown report as a new session, redirects to it.

Chat-with-Claude on the session detail view. Tool-use loop with six tools: list_remaining_overrides, get_override_file, get_core_file, apply_fix, dismiss_override, dismiss_all. Conversation persists turn-by-turn in a new messages LONGTEXT column on #__cstemplateintegrity_sessions.
Diagnostics modal with Test connection button (sends a tiny request to api.anthropic.com to verify the saved key authenticates and the network path works).
First-run disclaimer modal with "before you start" warning about backups and AI fallibility.
File backups view with restore action — every override patch is auto-backed-up to #__cstemplateintegrity_backups before the write, fully reversible.

fix.item: Dark-mode-friendly disclaimer modal — the modal styling now respects Atum's dark theme.

Hotfix: strip BOM that broke v1.0.2 install. PowerShell's Set-Content -Encoding utf8 adds a BOM by default in PS 5.1; the build script now detects BOM and fails the build before producing a broken zip.

addition.item: First-run disclaimer modal explaining the AI workflow and emphasizing the need for a recent backup.
Joomla 5+6 native support and a full Joomla update server (updates.xml on GitHub raw, downloads from GitHub release assets, SHA-256 verification on every download).
